comfortable ride for luxury passengers with high speed is the requirment. it rains alot so cockpit necessary, no cabin, no overnights. st thomas does not have the greatest of maintenance facilities so yamaha  ouboards preferred. 

----- Original Message ----
From: Kobus Potgieter <kobus@kobusnavaldesign.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tuesday, June 17, 2008 9:25:23 AM
Subject: Re: 12m RIB & Coast Guard version

My designs are used by the Turkish Coast Guard, Irish Coast Guard, Danish and by Zodiac International.
 The Black sea (Turkye) and around Ireland gets very rough and choppy, not to mention where I life in Cape Town, South Africa. Known as the Cape of Storms.

My boats works very well on rough / choppy seas. This is where most of my designs are used.
 

    Kobus Potgieter

On 15 Jun 2008, at 4:37 AM, J. Epstein wrote:

I am interested in purchasing your luxury 12, and the coast guard equivalent for the caribean.. please send details thanks

subject: Re: 12m RIB & Coast Guard version
body:
The 12m Coast Guard boat is powered by 3 x 300Hp outboard. Speeds reached is about 70 knots. Smaller OBM and twin installation can be fitted. With Twin 300 Hp, the vessel will reach speeds of approx 60 knots.
No vessels are in US. Turkish Coast Guard will be starting to produce this vessel in the next two months and will be manufactured in Istanbul. I have no idea on the production price at this stage.

However, if you hear of someone who would like to start producing this vessel in US, I'm open for discussion.



    Kobus Potgieter
      Naval Architect

K N D
KOBUS NAVAL DESIGN

 T : +27 21 4489464
 M: +27 825742160
 F:  +27 21 4479468
SKYPE: navaldesign1
kobus@kobusnavaldesign.com

www.kobusnavaldesign.com

My boats works very well on rough / choppy seas. This is where most of my designs are used.
